notes: Strict comparison covers customerMerge with non-empty normalized attached resources: customer-owned addresses, customer-owned metafields, source order transfer, customerByIdentifier lookup behavior, customers/customersCount tag search visibility, and job-status shape through the downstream merge read. The live fixture also records preview and IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED polling samples. Draft-order setup is captured, but customer-merge draft-order transfer remains deferred until a downstream draft-order read is captured.

expectedDifferences.reason: Customer merge jobs are allocated independently by Shopify and the local staging identity registry.
The downstream proxy read observes the deterministic local merge timestamp instead of Shopify's live write timestamp.
Shopify connection cursors are opaque and the local proxy uses deterministic synthetic cursors.
Shopify connection cursors are opaque and the local proxy uses deterministic synthetic cursors.
Shopify allocates a new metafield ID when copying a source-only customer metafield during merge; the local proxy allocates its own synthetic metafield ID.
Shopify connection cursors are opaque and the local proxy uses deterministic synthetic cursors.
Shopify connection cursors are opaque and the local proxy uses deterministic synthetic cursors and the copied source-only metafield receives a local ID.
The downstream proxy job-status read uses the locally generated merge job ID.
